verses.kjv: IN the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
verses.bsb: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.

verses.kjv: And the earth was without form, and void; and darkness was upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.
verses.bsb: Now the earth was formless and void, and darkness was over the surface of the deep. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the surface of the waters.

verses.kjv: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.
verses.bsb: And God said, “Let there be light,” and there was light.

verses.kjv: And God saw the light, that it was good: and God divided the light from the darkness.
verses.bsb: And God saw that the light was good, and He separated the light from the darkness.

verses.kjv: And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And the evening and the morning were the first day.
verses.bsb: God called the light “day,” and the darkness He called “night.”  And there was evening, and there was morning—the first day.

verses.kjv: ¶ And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.
verses.bsb: And God said, “Let there be an expanse between the waters, to separate the waters from the waters.”

verses.kjv: And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament: and it was so.
verses.bsb: So God made the expanse and separated the waters beneath it from the waters above. And it was so.

verses.kjv: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.
verses.bsb: God called the expanse “sky.”  And there was evening, and there was morning—the second day.

verses.kjv: ¶ And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.
verses.bsb: And God said, “Let the waters under the sky be gathered into one place, so that the dry land may appear.” And it was so.

verses.kjv: And God called the dry land Earth; and the gathering together of the waters called he Seas: and God saw that it was good.
verses.bsb: God called the dry land “earth,” and the gathering of waters He called “seas.” And God saw that it was good.

verses.kjv: And God said, Let the earth bring forth grass, the herb yielding seed, and the fruit tree yielding fruit after his kind, whose seed is in itself, upon the earth: and it was so.
verses.bsb: Then God said, “Let the earth bring forth vegetation: seed-bearing plants and fruit trees, each bearing fruit with seed according to its kind.” And it was so.

verses.kjv: And the earth brought forth grass, and herb yielding seed after his kind, and the tree yielding fruit, whose seed was in itself, after his kind: and God saw that it was good.
verses.bsb: The earth produced vegetation: seed-bearing plants according to their kinds and trees bearing fruit with seed according to their kinds. And God saw that it was good.

verses.kjv: And the evening and the morning were the third day.
verses.bsb: And there was evening, and there was morning—the third day.

verses.kjv: ¶ And God said, Let there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days, and years:
verses.bsb: And God said, “Let there be lights in the expanse of the sky to distinguish between the day and the night, and let them be signs to mark the seasons and days and years.

verses.kjv: And let them be for lights in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth: and it was so.
verses.bsb: And let them serve as lights in the expanse of the sky to shine upon the earth.” And it was so.

verses.kjv: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: he made the stars also.
verses.bsb: God made two great lights: the greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night. And He made the stars as well.

verses.kjv: And God set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth,
verses.bsb: God set these lights in the expanse of the sky to shine upon the earth,

verses.kjv: And to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness: and God saw that it was good.
verses.bsb: to preside over the day and the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. And God saw that it was good.

verses.kjv: And the evening and the morning were the fourth day.
verses.bsb: And there was evening, and there was morning—the fourth day.

verses.kjv: And God said, Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl that may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.
verses.bsb: And God said, “Let the waters teem with living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth in the open expanse of the sky.”

verses.kjv: And God created great whales, and every living creature that moveth, which the waters brought forth abundantly, after their kind, and every winged fowl after his kind: and God saw that it was good.
verses.bsb: So God created the great sea creatures and every living thing that moves, with which the waters teemed according to their kinds, and every bird of flight after its kind. And God saw that it was good.

verses.kjv: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
verses.bsb: Then God blessed them and said, “Be fruitful and multiply and fill the waters of the seas, and let birds multiply on the earth.”

verses.kjv: And the evening and the morning were the fifth day.
verses.bsb: And there was evening, and there was morning—the fifth day.

verses.kjv: ¶ And God said, Let the earth bring forth the living creature after his kind, cattle, and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after his kind: and it was so.
verses.bsb: And God said, “Let the earth bring forth living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, land crawlers, and beasts of the earth according to their kinds.” And it was so.

verses.kjv: And God made the beast of the earth after his kind, and cattle after their kind, and every thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind: and God saw that it was good.
verses.bsb: God made the beasts of the earth according to their kinds, the livestock according to their kinds, and everything that crawls upon the earth according to its kind. And God saw that it was good.

verses.kjv: ¶ And God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.
verses.bsb: Then God said, “Let Us make man in Our image, after Our likeness, to rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the air, over the livestock, and over all the earth itself and every creature that crawls upon it.”

verses.kjv: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.
verses.bsb: So God created man in His own image; in the image of God He created him; male and female He created them.

verses.kjv: And God blessed them, and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it: and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.
verses.bsb: God blessed them and said to them, “Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth and subdue it; rule over the fish of the sea and the birds of the air and every creature that crawls upon the earth.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed, which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in the which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for meat.
verses.bsb: Then God said, “Behold, I have given you every seed-bearing plant on the face of all the earth, and every tree whose fruit contains seed. They will be yours for food.

verses.kjv: And to every beast of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to every thing that creepeth upon the earth, wherein there is life, I have given every green herb for meat: and it was so.
verses.bsb: And to every beast of the earth and every bird of the air and every creature that crawls upon the earth—everything that has the breath of life in it—I have given every green plant for food.” And it was so.

verses.kjv: And God saw every thing that he had made, and, behold, it was very good. And the evening and the morning were the sixth day.
verses.bsb: And God looked upon all that He had made, and indeed, it was very good.  And there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.

verses.kjv: THUS the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them.
verses.bsb: Thus the heavens and the earth were completed in all their vast array.

verses.kjv: And on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made.
verses.bsb: And by the seventh day God had finished the work He had been doing; so on that day He rested from all His work.

verses.kjv: And God blessed the seventh day, and sanctified it: because that in it he had rested from all his work which God created and made.
verses.bsb: Then God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it, because on that day He rested from all the work of creation that He had accomplished.

verses.kjv: ¶ These are the generations of the heavens and of the earth when they were created, in the day that the Lord God made the earth and the heavens,
verses.bsb: This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created, in the day that the LORD God made them.

verses.kjv: And every plant of the field before it was in the earth, and every herb of the field before it grew: for the Lord God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was not a man to till the ground.
verses.bsb: Now no shrub of the field had yet appeared on the earth, nor had any plant of the field sprouted; for the LORD God had not yet sent rain upon the earth, and there was no man to cultivate the ground.

verses.kjv: But there went up a mist from the earth, and watered the whole face of the ground.
verses.bsb: But springs welled up from the earth and watered the whole surface of the ground.

verses.kjv: And the Lord God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD God formed man from the dust of the ground and breathed the breath of life into his nostrils, and the man became a living being.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord God planted a garden eastward in Eden; and there he put the man whom he had formed.
verses.bsb: And the LORD God planted a garden in Eden, in the east, where He placed the man He had formed.

verses.kjv: And out of the ground made the Lord God to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.
verses.bsb: Out of the ground the LORD God gave growth to every tree that is pleasing to the eye and good for food. And in the middle of the garden were the tree of life and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.

verses.kjv: And a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from thence it was parted, and became into four heads.
verses.bsb: Now a river flowed out of Eden to water the garden, and from there it branched into four headwaters:

verses.kjv: The name of the first is Pison: that is it which compasseth the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;
verses.bsb: The name of the first river is Pishon; it winds through the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold.

verses.kjv: And the gold of that land is good: there is bdellium and the onyx stone.
verses.bsb: And the gold of that land is pure, and bdellium and onyx are found there.

verses.kjv: And the name of the second river is Gihon: the same is it that compasseth the whole land of Ethiopia.
verses.bsb: The name of the second river is Gihon; it winds through the whole land of Cush.

verses.kjv: And the name of the third river is Hiddekel: that is it which goeth toward the east of Assyria. And the fourth river is Euphrates.
verses.bsb: The name of the third river is Hiddekel; it runs along the east side of Assyria.   And the fourth river is the Euphrates.

verses.kjv: And the Lord God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD God took the man and placed him in the Garden of Eden to cultivate and keep it.

verses.kjv: And the Lord God commanded the man, saying, Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat:
verses.bsb: And the LORD God commanded him, “You may eat freely from every tree of the garden,

verses.kjv: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.
verses.bsb: but you must not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil; for in the day that you eat of it, you will surely die.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord God said, It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him.
verses.bsb: The LORD God also said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make for him a suitable helper.”

verses.kjv: And out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that was the name thereof.
verses.bsb: And out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the air, and He brought them to the man to see what he would name each one. And whatever the man called each living creature, that was its name.

verses.kjv: And Adam gave names to all cattle, and to the fowl of the air, and to every beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found an help meet for him.
verses.bsb: The man gave names to all the livestock, to the birds of the air, and to every beast of the field. But for Adam no suitable helper was found.

verses.kjv: And the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam, and he slept: and he took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof;
verses.bsb: So the LORD God caused the man to fall into a deep sleep, and while he slept, He took one of the man’s ribs and closed up the area with flesh.

verses.kjv: And the rib, which the Lord God had taken from man, made he a woman, and brought her unto the man.
verses.bsb: And from the rib that the LORD God had taken from the man, He made a woman and brought her to him.

verses.kjv: And Adam said, This is now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.
verses.bsb: And the man said:  “This is now bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called ‘woman,’ for out of man she was taken.”

verses.kjv: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
verses.bsb: For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and they will become one flesh.

verses.kjv: And they were both naked, the man and his wife, and were not ashamed.
verses.bsb: And the man and his wife were both naked, and they were not ashamed.

verses.kjv: NOW the serpent was more subtil than any beast of the field which the Lord God had made. And he said unto the woman, Yea, hath God said, Ye shall not eat of every tree of the garden?
verses.bsb: Now the serpent was more crafty than any beast of the field that the LORD God had made. And he said to the woman, “Did God really say, ‘You must not eat from any tree in the garden?’”

verses.kjv: And the woman said unto the serpent, We may eat of the fruit of the trees of the garden:
verses.bsb: The woman answered the serpent, “We may eat the fruit of the trees of the garden,

verses.kjv: But of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden, God hath said, Ye shall not eat of it, neither shall ye touch it, lest ye die.
verses.bsb: but about the fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden, God has said, ‘You must not eat of it or touch it, or you will die.’”

verses.kjv: And the serpent said unto the woman, Ye shall not surely die:
verses.bsb: “You will not surely die,” the serpent told her.

verses.kjv: For God doth know that in the day ye eat thereof, then your eyes shall be opened, and ye shall be as gods, knowing good and evil.
verses.bsb: “For God knows that in the day you eat of it, your eyes will be opened and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.”

verses.kjv: And when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was pleasant to the eyes, and a tree to be desired to make one wise, she took of the fruit thereof, and did eat, and gave also unto her husband with her; and he did eat.
verses.bsb: When the woman saw that the tree was good for food and pleasing to the eyes, and that it was desirable for obtaining wisdom, she took the fruit and ate it. She also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate it.

verses.kjv: And the eyes of them both were opened, and they knew that they were naked; and they sewed fig leaves together, and made themselves aprons.
verses.bsb: And the eyes of both of them were opened, and they knew that they were naked; so they sewed together fig leaves and made coverings for themselves.

verses.kjv: And they heard the voice of the Lord God walking in the garden in the cool of the day: and Adam and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the Lord God amongst the trees of the garden.
verses.bsb: Then the man and his wife heard the voice of the LORD God walking in the garden in the breeze of the day, and they hid themselves from the presence of the LORD God among the trees of the garden.

verses.kjv: And the Lord God called unto Adam, and said unto him, Where art thou?
verses.bsb: But the LORD God called out to the man, “Where are you?”

verses.kjv: And he said, I heard thy voice in the garden, and I was afraid, because I was naked; and I hid myself.
verses.bsb: “I heard Your voice in the garden,” he replied, “and I was afraid because I was naked; so I hid myself.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Who told thee that thou wast naked? Hast thou eaten of the tree, whereof I commanded thee that thou shouldest not eat?
verses.bsb: “Who told you that you were naked?” asked the LORD God. “Have you eaten from the tree of which I commanded you not to eat?”

verses.kjv: And the man said, The woman whom thou gavest to be with me, she gave me of the tree, and I did eat.
verses.bsb: And the man answered, “The woman whom You gave me, she gave me fruit from the tree, and I ate it.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord God said unto the woman, What is this that thou hast done? And the woman said, The serpent beguiled me, and I did eat.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD God said to the woman, “What is this you have done?”  “The serpent deceived me,” she replied, “and I ate.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord God said unto the serpent, Because thou hast done this, thou art cursed above all cattle, and above every beast of the field; upon thy belly shalt thou go, and dust shalt thou eat all the days of thy life:
verses.bsb: So the LORD God said to the serpent:  “Because you have done this, cursed are you above all livestock and every beast of the field! On your belly will you go, and dust you will eat, all the days of your life.

verses.kjv: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
verses.bsb: And I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your seed and her seed. He will crush your head, and you will strike his heel.”

verses.kjv: Unto the woman he said, I will greatly multiply thy sorrow and thy conception; in sorrow thou shalt bring forth children; and thy desire shall be to thy husband, and he shall rule over thee.
verses.bsb: To the woman He said:  “I will sharply increase your pain in childbirth; in pain you will bring forth children. Your desire will be for your husband, and he will rule over you.”

verses.kjv: And unto Adam he said, Because thou hast hearkened unto the voice of thy wife, and hast eaten of the tree, of which I commanded thee, saying, Thou shalt not eat of it: cursed is the ground for thy sake; in sorrow shalt thou eat of it all the days of thy life;
verses.bsb: And to Adam He said:  “Because you have listened to the voice of your wife and have eaten from the tree of which I commanded you not to eat, cursed is the ground because of you; through toil you will eat of it all the days of your life.

verses.kjv: Thorns also and thistles shall it bring forth to thee; and thou shalt eat the herb of the field;
verses.bsb: Both thorns and thistles it will yield for you, and you will eat the plants of the field.

verses.kjv: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground; for out of it wast thou taken: for dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return.
verses.bsb: By the sweat of your brow you will eat your bread, until you return to the ground— because out of it were you taken. For dust you are, and to dust you shall return.”

verses.kjv: And Adam called his wife’s name Eve; because she was the mother of all living.
verses.bsb: And Adam named his wife Eve, because she would be the mother of all the living.

verses.kjv: Unto Adam also and to his wife did the Lord God make coats of skins, and clothed them.
verses.bsb: And the LORD God made garments of skin for Adam and his wife, and He clothed them.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord God said, Behold, the man is become as one of us, to know good and evil: and now, lest he put forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever:
verses.bsb: Then the LORD God said, “Behold, the man has become like one of Us, knowing good and evil. And now, lest he reach out his hand and take also from the tree of life, and eat, and live forever...”

verses.kjv: Therefore the Lord God sent him forth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from whence he was taken.
verses.bsb: Therefore the LORD God banished him from the Garden of Eden to work the ground from which he had been taken.

verses.kjv: So he drove out the man; and he placed at the east of the garden of Eden Cherubims, and a flaming sword which turned every way, to keep the way of the tree of life.
verses.bsb: So He drove out the man and stationed cherubim on the east side of the Garden of Eden, along with a whirling sword of flame to guard the way to the tree of life.

verses.kjv: AND Adam knew Eve his wife; and she conceived, and bare Cain, and said, I have gotten a man from the Lord.
verses.bsb: And Adam had relations with his wife Eve, and she conceived and gave birth to Cain.  “With the help of the LORD I have brought forth a man,” she said.

verses.kjv: And she again bare his brother Abel. And Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.
verses.bsb: Later she gave birth to Cain’s brother Abel.  Now Abel was a keeper of sheep, while Cain was a tiller of the soil.

verses.kjv: And in process of time it came to pass, that Cain brought of the fruit of the ground an offering unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: So in the course of time, Cain brought some of the fruit of the soil as an offering to the LORD,

verses.kjv: And Abel, he also brought of the firstlings of his flock and of the fat thereof. And the Lord had respect unto Abel and to his offering:
verses.bsb: while Abel brought the best portions of the firstborn of his flock.  And the LORD looked with favor on Abel and his offering,

verses.kjv: But unto Cain and to his offering he had not respect. And Cain was very wroth, and his countenance fell.
verses.bsb: but He had no regard for Cain and his offering. So Cain became very angry, and his countenance fell.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto Cain, Why art thou wroth? and why is thy countenance fallen?
verses.bsb: “Why are you angry,” said the LORD to Cain, “and why has your countenance fallen?

verses.kjv: If thou doest well, shalt thou not be accepted? and if thou doest not well, sin lieth at the door. And unto thee shall be his desire, and thou shalt rule over him.
verses.bsb: If you do what is right, will you not be accepted? But if you refuse to do what is right, sin is crouching at your door; it desires you, but you must master it.”

verses.kjv: And Cain talked with Abel his brother: and it came to pass, when they were in the field, that Cain rose up against Abel his brother, and slew him.
verses.bsb: Then Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let us go out to the field.” And while they were in the field, Cain rose up against his brother Abel and killed him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Cain, Where is Abel thy brother? And he said, I know not: Am I my brother’s keeper?
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Cain, “Where is your brother Abel?”  “I do not know!” he answered. “Am I my brother’s keeper?”

verses.kjv: And he said, What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother’s blood crieth unto me from the ground.
verses.bsb: “What have you done?” replied the LORD. “The voice of your brother’s blood cries out to Me from the ground.

verses.kjv: And now art thou cursed from the earth, which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brother’s blood from thy hand;
verses.bsb: Now you are cursed and banished from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood from your hand.

verses.kjv: When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength; a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth.
verses.bsb: When you till the ground, it will no longer yield its produce to you. You will be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth.”

verses.kjv: And Cain said unto the Lord, My punishment is greater than I can bear.
verses.bsb: But Cain said to the LORD, “My punishment is greater than I can bear.

verses.kjv: Behold, thou hast driven me out this day from the face of the earth; and from thy face shall I be hid; and I shall be a fugitive and a vagabond in the earth; and it shall come to pass, that every one that findeth me shall slay me.
verses.bsb: Behold, this day You have driven me from the face of the earth, and from Your face I will be hidden; I will be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and whoever finds me will kill me.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto him, Therefore whosoever slayeth Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold. And the Lord set a mark upon Cain, lest any finding him should kill him.
verses.bsb: “Not so!” replied the LORD. “If anyone slays Cain, then Cain will be avenged sevenfold.” And the LORD placed a mark on Cain, so that no one who found him would kill him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Cain went out from the presence of the Lord, and dwelt in the land of Nod, on the east of Eden.
verses.bsb: So Cain went out from the presence of the LORD and settled in the land of Nod, east of Eden.

verses.kjv: And Cain knew his wife; and she conceived, and bare Enoch: and he builded a city, and called the name of the city, after the name of his son, Enoch.
verses.bsb: And Cain had relations with his wife, and she conceived and gave birth to Enoch. Then Cain built a city and named it after his son Enoch.

verses.kjv: And unto Enoch was born Irad: and Irad begat Mehujael: and Mehujael begat Methusael: and Methusael begat Lamech.
verses.bsb: Now to Enoch was born Irad, and Irad was the father of Mehujael, and Mehujael was the father of Methusael, and Methusael was the father of Lamech.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Lamech took unto him two wives: the name of the one was Adah, and the name of the other Zillah.
verses.bsb: And Lamech married two women, one named Adah and the other Zillah.

verses.kjv: And Adah bare Jabal: he was the father of such as dwell in tents, and of such as have cattle.
verses.bsb: Adah gave birth to Jabal; he was the father of those who dwell in tents and raise livestock.

verses.kjv: And his brother’s name was Jubal: he was the father of all such as handle the harp and organ.
verses.bsb: And his brother’s name was Jubal; he was the father of all who play the harp and flute.

verses.kjv: And Zillah, she also bare Tubal-cain, an instructer of every artificer in brass and iron: and the sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.
verses.bsb: And Zillah gave birth to Tubal-cain, a forger of every implement of bronze and iron. And the sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.

verses.kjv: And Lamech said unto his wives, Adah and Zillah, Hear my voice; ye wives of Lamech, hearken unto my speech: for I have slain a man to my wounding, and a young man to my hurt.
verses.bsb: Then Lamech said to his wives:  “Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; wives of Lamech, listen to my speech. For I have slain a man for wounding me, a young man for striking me.

verses.kjv: If Cain shall be avenged sevenfold, truly Lamech seventy and sevenfold.
verses.bsb: If Cain is avenged sevenfold, then Lamech seventy-sevenfold.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Adam knew his wife again; and she bare a son, and called his name Seth: For God, said she, hath appointed me another seed instead of Abel, whom Cain slew.
verses.bsb: And Adam again had relations with his wife, and she gave birth to a son and named him Seth, saying, “God has granted me another seed in place of Abel, since Cain killed him.”

verses.kjv: And to Seth, to him also there was born a son; and he called his name Enos: then began men to call upon the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: And to Seth also a son was born, and he called him Enosh.  At that time men began to call upon the name of the LORD.

verses.kjv: THIS is the book of the generations of Adam. In the day that God created man, in the likeness of God made he him;
verses.bsb: This is the book of the generations of Adam. In the day that God created man, He made him in His own likeness.

verses.kjv: Male and female created he them; and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created.
verses.bsb: Male and female He created them, and He blessed them. And in the day they were created, He called them “man.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Adam lived an hundred and thirty years, and begat a son in his own likeness, after his image; and called his name Seth:
verses.bsb: When Adam was 130 years old, he had a son in his own likeness, after his own image; and he named him Seth.

verses.kjv: And the days of Adam after he had begotten Seth were eight hundred years: and he begat sons and daughters:
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Seth, Adam lived 800 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years: and he died.
verses.bsb: So Adam lived a total of 930 years, and then he died.

verses.kjv: And Seth lived an hundred and five years, and begat Enos:
verses.bsb: When Seth was 105 years old, he became the father of Enosh.

verses.kjv: And Seth lived after he begat Enos eight hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters:
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Enosh, Seth lived 807 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years: and he died.
verses.bsb: So Seth lived a total of 912 years, and then he died.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Enos lived ninety years, and begat Cainan:
verses.bsb: When Enosh was 90 years old, he became the father of Kenan.

verses.kjv: And Enos lived after he begat Cainan eight hundred and fifteen years, and begat sons and daughters:
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Kenan, Enosh lived 815 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And all the days of Enos were nine hundred and five years: and he died.
verses.bsb: So Enosh lived a total of 905 years, and then he died.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Cainan lived seventy years, and begat Mahalaleel:
verses.bsb: When Kenan was 70 years old, he became the father of Mahalalel.

verses.kjv: And Cainan lived after he begat Mahalaleel eight hundred and forty years, and begat sons and daughters:
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Mahalalel, Kenan lived 840 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And all the days of Cainan were nine hundred and ten years: and he died.
verses.bsb: So Kenan lived a total of 910 years, and then he died.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Mahalaleel lived sixty and five years, and begat Jared:
verses.bsb: When Mahalalel was 65 years old, he became the father of Jared.

verses.kjv: And Mahalaleel lived after he begat Jared eight hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters:
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Jared, Mahalalel lived 830 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And all the days of Mahalaleel were eight hundred ninety and five years: and he died.
verses.bsb: So Mahalalel lived a total of 895 years, and then he died.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Jared lived an hundred sixty and two years, and he begat Enoch:
verses.bsb: When Jared was 162 years old, he became the father of Enoch.

verses.kjv: And Jared lived after he begat Enoch eight hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Enoch, Jared lived 800 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And all the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty and two years: and he died.
verses.bsb: So Jared lived a total of 962 years, and then he died.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Enoch lived sixty and five years, and begat Methuselah:
verses.bsb: When Enoch was 65 years old, he became the father of Methuselah.

verses.kjv: And Enoch walked with God after he begat Methuselah three hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Methuselah, Enoch walked with God 300 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And all the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty and five years:
verses.bsb: So Enoch lived a total of 365 years.

verses.kjv: And Enoch walked with God: and he was not; for God took him.
verses.bsb: Enoch walked with God, and then he was no more, because God had taken him away.

verses.kjv: And Methuselah lived an hundred eighty and seven years, and begat Lamech:
verses.bsb: When Methuselah was 187 years old, he became the father of Lamech.

verses.kjv: And Methuselah lived after he begat Lamech seven hundred eighty and two years, and begat sons and daughters:
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Lamech, Methuselah lived 782 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred sixty and nine years: and he died.
verses.bsb: So Methuselah lived a total of 969 years, and then he died.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Lamech lived an hundred eighty and two years, and begat a son:
verses.bsb: When Lamech was 182 years old, he had a son.

verses.kjv: And he called his name Noah, saying, This same shall comfort us concerning our work and toil of our hands, because of the ground which the Lord hath cursed.
verses.bsb: And he named him Noah, saying, “May this one comfort us in the labor and toil of our hands caused by the ground that the LORD has cursed.”

verses.kjv: And Lamech lived after he begat Noah five hundred ninety and five years, and begat sons and daughters:
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Noah, Lamech lived 595 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And all the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy and seven years: and he died.
verses.bsb: So Lamech lived a total of 777 years, and then he died.

verses.kjv: And Noah was five hundred years old: and Noah begat Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
verses.bsb: After Noah was 500 years old, he became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass, when men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born unto them,
verses.bsb: Now when men began to multiply on the face of the earth and daughters were born to them,

verses.kjv: That the sons of God saw the daughters of men that they were fair; and they took them wives of all which they chose.
verses.bsb: the sons of God saw that the daughters of men were beautiful, and they took as wives whomever they chose.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said, My spirit shall not always strive with man, for that he also is flesh: yet his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.
verses.bsb: So the LORD said, “My Spirit will not contend with man forever, for he is mortal; his days shall be 120 years.”

verses.kjv: There were giants in the earth in those days; and also after that, when the sons of God came in unto the daughters of men, and they bare children to them, the same became mighty men which were of old, men of renown.
verses.bsb: The Nephilim were on the earth in those days—and afterward as well—when the sons of God had relations with the daughters of men. And they bore them children who became the mighty men of old, men of renown.

verses.kjv: ¶ And God saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD saw that the wickedness of man was great upon the earth, and that every inclination of the thoughts of his heart was altogether evil all the time.

verses.kjv: And it repented the Lord that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him at his heart.
verses.bsb: And the LORD regretted that He had made man on the earth, and He was grieved in His heart.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said, I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth; both man, and beast, and the creeping thing, and the fowls of the air; for it repenteth me that I have made them.
verses.bsb: So the LORD said, “I will blot out man, whom I have created, from the face of the earth—every man and beast and crawling creature and bird of the air—for I am grieved that I have made them.”

verses.kjv: But Noah found grace in the eyes of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Noah, however, found favor in the eyes of the LORD.

verses.kjv: ¶ These are the generations of Noah: Noah was a just man and perfect in his generations, and Noah walked with God.
verses.bsb: This is the account of Noah. Noah was a righteous man, blameless in his generation; Noah walked with God.

verses.kjv: And Noah begat three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
verses.bsb: And Noah had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

verses.kjv: The earth also was corrupt before God, and the earth was filled with violence.
verses.bsb: Now the earth was corrupt in the sight of God, and full of violence.

verses.kjv: And God looked upon the earth, and, behold, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted his way upon the earth.
verses.bsb: And God looked upon the earth and saw that it was corrupt; for all living creatures on the earth had corrupted their ways.

verses.kjv: And God said unto Noah, The end of all flesh is come before me; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
verses.bsb: Then God said to Noah, “The end of all living creatures has come before Me, because through them the earth is full of violence. Now behold, I will destroy both them and the earth.

verses.kjv: ¶ Make thee an ark of gopher wood; rooms shalt thou make in the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch.
verses.bsb: Make for yourself an ark of gopher wood; make rooms in the ark and coat it with pitch inside and out.

verses.kjv: And this is the fashion which thou shalt make it of: The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits, the breadth of it fifty cubits, and the height of it thirty cubits.
verses.bsb: And this is how you are to build it: The ark is to be 300 cubits long, 50 cubits wide, and 30 cubits high.

verses.kjv: A window shalt thou make to the ark, and in a cubit shalt thou finish it above; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; with lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.
verses.bsb: You are to make a roof for the ark, finish its walls a cubit from the top, place a door in the side of the ark, and build lower, middle, and upper decks.

verses.kjv: And, behold, I, even I, do bring a flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein is the breath of life, from under heaven; and every thing that is in the earth shall die.
verses.bsb: And behold, I will bring floodwaters upon the earth to destroy every creature under the heavens that has the breath of life. Everything on the earth will perish.

verses.kjv: But with thee will I establish my covenant; and thou shalt come into the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy sons’ wives with thee.
verses.bsb: But I will establish My covenant with you, and you will enter the ark—you and your sons and your wife and your sons’ wives with you.

verses.kjv: And of every living thing of all flesh, two of every sort shalt thou bring into the ark, to keep them alive with thee; they shall be male and female.
verses.bsb: And you are to bring two of every living creature into the ark—male and female—to keep them alive with you.

verses.kjv: Of fowls after their kind, and of cattle after their kind, of every creeping thing of the earth after his kind, two of every sort shall come unto thee, to keep them alive.
verses.bsb: Two of every kind of bird and animal and crawling creature will come to you to be kept alive.

verses.kjv: And take thou unto thee of all food that is eaten, and thou shalt gather it to thee; and it shall be for food for thee, and for them.
verses.bsb: You are also to take for yourself every kind of food that is eaten and gather it as food for yourselves and for the animals.”

verses.kjv: Thus did Noah; according to all that God commanded him, so did he.
verses.bsb: So Noah did everything precisely as God had commanded him.

verses.kjv: AND the Lord said unto Noah, Come thou and all thy house into the ark; for thee have I seen righteous before me in this generation.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Noah, “Go into the ark, you and all your family, because I have found you righteous in this generation.

verses.kjv: Of every clean beast thou shalt take to thee by sevens, the male and his female: and of beasts that are not clean by two, the male and his female.
verses.bsb: You are to take with you seven pairs of every kind of clean animal, a male and its mate; a pair of every kind of unclean animal, a male and its mate;

verses.kjv: Of fowls also of the air by sevens, the male and the female; to keep seed alive upon the face of all the earth.
verses.bsb: and seven pairs of every kind of bird of the air, male and female, to preserve their offspring on the face of all the earth.

verses.kjv: For yet seven days, and I will cause it to rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights; and every living substance that I have made will I destroy from off the face of the earth.
verses.bsb: For seven days from now I will send rain on the earth for forty days and forty nights, and I will wipe from the face of the earth every living thing I have made.”

verses.kjv: And Noah did according unto all that the Lord commanded him.
verses.bsb: And Noah did all that the LORD had commanded him.

verses.kjv: And Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters was upon the earth.
verses.bsb: Now Noah was 600 years old when the floodwaters came upon the earth.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Noah went in, and his sons, and his wife, and his sons’ wives with him, into the ark, because of the waters of the flood.
verses.bsb: And Noah and his wife, with his sons and their wives, entered the ark to escape the waters of the flood.

verses.kjv: Of clean beasts, and of beasts that are not clean, and of fowls, and of every thing that creepeth upon the earth,
verses.bsb: The clean and unclean animals, the birds, and everything that crawls along the ground

verses.kjv: There went in two and two unto Noah into the ark, the male and the female, as God had commanded Noah.
verses.bsb: came to Noah to enter the ark, two by two, male and female, as God had commanded Noah.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass after seven days, that the waters of the flood were upon the earth.
verses.bsb: And after seven days the floodwaters came upon the earth.

verses.kjv: ¶ In the six hundredth year of Noah’s life, in the second month, the seventeenth day of the month, the same day were all the fountains of the great deep broken up, and the windows of heaven were opened.
verses.bsb: In the six hundredth year of Noah’s life, on the seventeenth day of the second month, all the fountains of the great deep burst forth, and the floodgates of the heavens were opened.

verses.kjv: And the rain was upon the earth forty days and forty nights.
verses.bsb: And the rain fell upon the earth for forty days and forty nights.

verses.kjv: In the selfsame day entered Noah, and Shem, and Ham, and Japheth, the sons of Noah, and Noah’s wife, and the three wives of his sons with them, into the ark;
verses.bsb: On that very day Noah entered the ark, along with his sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and his wife, and the three wives of his sons—

verses.kjv: They, and every beast after his kind, and all the cattle after their kind, and every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind, and every fowl after his kind, every bird of every sort.
verses.bsb: they and every kind of wild animal, livestock, crawling creature, bird, and winged creature.

verses.kjv: And they went in unto Noah into the ark, two and two of all flesh, wherein is the breath of life.
verses.bsb: They came to Noah to enter the ark, two by two of every creature with the breath of life.

verses.kjv: And they that went in, went in male and female of all flesh, as God had commanded him: and the Lord shut him in.
verses.bsb: And they entered, the male and female of every living thing, as God had commanded Noah. Then the LORD shut him in.

verses.kjv: And the flood was forty days upon the earth; and the waters increased, and bare up the ark, and it was lift up above the earth.
verses.bsb: For forty days the flood kept coming on the earth, and the waters rose and lifted the ark high above the earth.

verses.kjv: And the waters prevailed, and were increased greatly upon the earth; and the ark went upon the face of the waters.
verses.bsb: So the waters continued to surge and rise greatly on the earth, and the ark floated on the surface of the waters.

verses.kjv: And the waters prevailed exceedingly upon the earth; and all the high hills, that were under the whole heaven, were covered.
verses.bsb: Finally, the waters completely inundated the earth, so that all the high mountains under all the heavens were covered.

verses.kjv: Fifteen cubits upward did the waters prevail; and the mountains were covered.
verses.bsb: The waters rose and covered the mountaintops to a depth of fifteen cubits.

verses.kjv: And all flesh died that moved upon the earth, both of fowl, and of cattle, and of beast, and of every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth, and every man:
verses.bsb: And every living thing that moved upon the earth perished—birds, livestock, animals, every creature that swarms upon the earth, and all mankind.

verses.kjv: All in whose nostrils was the breath of life, of all that was in the dry land, died.
verses.bsb: Of all that was on dry land, everything that had the breath of life in its nostrils died.

verses.kjv: And every living substance was destroyed which was upon the face of the ground, both man, and cattle, and the creeping things, and the fowl of the heaven; and they were destroyed from the earth: and Noah only remained alive, and they that were with him in the ark.
verses.bsb: And every living thing on the face of the earth was destroyed—man and livestock, crawling creatures and birds of the air; they were blotted out from the earth, and only Noah and those with him in the ark remained.

verses.kjv: And the waters prevailed upon the earth an hundred and fifty days.
verses.bsb: And the waters prevailed upon the earth for 150 days.

verses.kjv: AND God remembered Noah, and every living thing, and all the cattle that was with him in the ark: and God made a wind to pass over the earth, and the waters asswaged;
verses.bsb: But God remembered Noah and all the animals and livestock that were with him in the ark. And God sent a wind over the earth, and the waters began to subside.

verses.kjv: The fountains also of the deep and the windows of heaven were stopped, and the rain from heaven was restrained;
verses.bsb: The springs of the deep and the floodgates of the heavens were closed, and the rain from the sky was restrained.

verses.kjv: And the waters returned from off the earth continually: and after the end of the hundred and fifty days the waters were abated.
verses.bsb: The waters receded steadily from the earth, and after 150 days the waters had gone down.

verses.kjv: And the ark rested in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, upon the mountains of Ararat.
verses.bsb: On the seventeenth day of the seventh month, the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.

verses.kjv: And the waters decreased continually until the tenth month: in the tenth month, on the first day of the month, were the tops of the mountains seen.
verses.bsb: And the waters continued to recede until the tenth month, and on the first day of the tenth month the tops of the mountains became visible.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass at the end of forty days, that Noah opened the window of the ark which he had made:
verses.bsb: After forty days Noah opened the window he had made in the ark

verses.kjv: And he sent forth a raven, which went forth to and fro, until the waters were dried up from off the earth.
verses.bsb: and sent out a raven. It kept flying back and forth until the waters had dried up from the earth.

verses.kjv: Also he sent forth a dove from him, to see if the waters were abated from off the face of the ground;
verses.bsb: Then Noah sent out a dove to see if the waters had receded from the surface of the ground.

verses.kjv: But the dove found no rest for the sole of her foot, and she returned unto him into the ark, for the waters were on the face of the whole earth: then he put forth his hand, and took her, and pulled her in unto him into the ark.
verses.bsb: But the dove found no place to rest her foot, and she returned to him in the ark, because the waters were still covering the surface of all the earth. So he reached out his hand and brought her back inside the ark.

verses.kjv: And he stayed yet other seven days; and again he sent forth the dove out of the ark;
verses.bsb: Noah waited seven more days and again sent out the dove from the ark.

verses.kjv: And the dove came in to him in the evening; and, lo, in her mouth was an olive leaf pluckt off: so Noah knew that the waters were abated from off the earth.
verses.bsb: And behold, the dove returned to him in the evening with a freshly plucked olive leaf in her beak. So Noah knew that the waters had receded from the earth.

verses.kjv: And he stayed yet other seven days; and sent forth the dove; which returned not again unto him any more.
verses.bsb: And Noah waited seven more days and sent out the dove again, but this time she did not return to him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass in the six hundredth and first year, in the first month, the first day of the month, the waters were dried up from off the earth: and Noah removed the covering of the ark, and looked, and, behold, the face of the ground was dry.
verses.bsb: In Noah’s six hundred and first year, on the first day of the first month, the waters had dried up from the earth. So Noah removed the covering from the ark and saw that the surface of the ground was dry.

verses.kjv: And in the second month, on the seven and twentieth day of the month, was the earth dried.
verses.bsb: By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was fully dry.

verses.kjv: ¶ And God spake unto Noah, saying,
verses.bsb: Then God said to Noah,

verses.kjv: Go forth of the ark, thou, and thy wife, and thy sons, and thy sons’ wives with thee.
verses.bsb: “Come out of the ark, you and your wife, along with your sons and their wives.

verses.kjv: Bring forth with thee every living thing that is with thee, of all flesh, both of fowl, and of cattle, and of every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth; that they may breed abundantly in the earth, and be fruitful, and multiply upon the earth.
verses.bsb: Bring out all the living creatures that are with you—birds, livestock, and everything that crawls upon the ground—so that they can spread out over the earth and be fruitful and multiply upon it.”

verses.kjv: And Noah went forth, and his sons, and his wife, and his sons’ wives with him:
verses.bsb: So Noah came out, along with his sons and his wife and his sons’ wives.

verses.kjv: Every beast, every creeping thing, and every fowl, and whatsoever creepeth upon the earth, after their kinds, went forth out of the ark.
verses.bsb: Every living creature, every creeping thing, and every bird—everything that moves upon the earth—came out of the ark, kind by kind.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Noah builded an altar unto the Lord; and took of every clean beast, and of every clean fowl, and offered burnt offerings on the altar.
verses.bsb: Then Noah built an altar to the LORD. And taking from every kind of clean animal and clean bird, he offered burnt offerings on the altar.

verses.kjv: And the Lord smelled a sweet savour; and the Lord said in his heart, I will not again curse the ground any more for man’s sake; for the imagination of man’s heart is evil from his youth; neither will I again smite any more every thing living, as I have done.
verses.bsb: When the LORD smelled the pleasing aroma, He said in His heart, “Never again will I curse the ground because of man, even though every inclination of his heart is evil from his youth. And never again will I destroy all living creatures as I have done.

verses.kjv: While the earth remaineth, seedtime and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease.
verses.bsb: As long as the earth endures, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night shall never cease.”

verses.kjv: AND God blessed Noah and his sons, and said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth.
verses.bsb: And God blessed Noah and his sons and said to them, “Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth.

verses.kjv: And the fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth, and upon every fowl of the air, upon all that moveth upon the earth, and upon all the fishes of the sea; into your hand are they delivered.
verses.bsb: The fear and dread of you will fall on every living creature on the earth, every bird of the air, every creature that crawls on the ground, and all the fish of the sea. They are delivered into your hand.

verses.kjv: Every moving thing that liveth shall be meat for you; even as the green herb have I given you all things.
verses.bsb: Everything that lives and moves will be food for you; just as I gave you the green plants, I now give you all things.

verses.kjv: But flesh with the life thereof, which is the blood thereof, shall ye not eat.
verses.bsb: But you must not eat meat with its lifeblood still in it.

verses.kjv: And surely your blood of your lives will I require; at the hand of every beast will I require it, and at the hand of man; at the hand of every man’s brother will I require the life of man.
verses.bsb: And surely I will require the life of any man or beast by whose hand your lifeblood is shed. I will demand an accounting from anyone who takes the life of his fellow man:

verses.kjv: Whoso sheddeth man’s blood, by man shall his blood be shed: for in the image of God made he man.
verses.bsb: Whoever sheds the blood of man, by man his blood will be shed; for in His own image God has made mankind.

verses.kjv: And you, be ye fruitful, and multiply; bring forth abundantly in the earth, and multiply therein.
verses.bsb: But as for you, be fruitful and multiply; spread out across the earth and multiply upon it.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And God spake unto Noah, and to his sons with him, saying,
verses.bsb: Then God said to Noah and his sons with him,

verses.kjv: And I, behold, I establish my covenant with you, and with your seed after you;
verses.bsb: “Behold, I now establish My covenant with you and your descendants after you,

verses.kjv: And with every living creature that is with you, of the fowl, of the cattle, and of every beast of the earth with you; from all that go out of the ark, to every beast of the earth.
verses.bsb: and with every living creature that was with you—the birds, the livestock, and every beast of the earth—every living thing that came out of the ark.

verses.kjv: And I will establish my covenant with you; neither shall all flesh be cut off any more by the waters of a flood; neither shall there any more be a flood to destroy the earth.
verses.bsb: And I establish My covenant with you: Never again will all life be cut off by the waters of a flood; never again will there be a flood to destroy the earth.”

verses.kjv: And God said, This is the token of the covenant which I make between me and you and every living creature that is with you, for perpetual generations:
verses.bsb: And God said, “This is the sign of the covenant I am making between Me and you and every living creature with you, a covenant for all generations to come:

verses.kjv: I do set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be for a token of a covenant between me and the earth.
verses.bsb: I have set My rainbow in the clouds, and it will be a sign of the covenant between Me and the earth.

verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, when I bring a cloud over the earth, that the bow shall be seen in the cloud:
verses.bsb: Whenever I form clouds over the earth and the rainbow appears in the clouds,

verses.kjv: And I will remember my covenant, which is between me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and the waters shall no more become a flood to destroy all flesh.
verses.bsb: I will remember My covenant between Me and you and every living creature of every kind. Never again will the waters become a flood to destroy all life.

verses.kjv: And the bow shall be in the cloud; and I will look upon it, that I may remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is upon the earth.
verses.bsb: And whenever the rainbow appears in the clouds, I will see it and remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of every kind that is on the earth.”

verses.kjv: And God said unto Noah, This is the token of the covenant, which I have established between me and all flesh that is upon the earth.
verses.bsb: So God said to Noah, “This is the sign of the covenant that I have established between Me and every creature on the earth.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the sons of Noah, that went forth of the ark, were Shem, and Ham, and Japheth: and Ham is the father of Canaan.
verses.bsb: The sons of Noah who came out of the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. And Ham was the father of Canaan.

verses.kjv: These are the three sons of Noah: and of them was the whole earth overspread.
verses.bsb: These three were the sons of Noah, and from them the whole earth was populated.

verses.kjv: And Noah began to be an husbandman, and he planted a vineyard:
verses.bsb: Now Noah, a man of the soil, proceeded to plant a vineyard.

verses.kjv: And he drank of the wine, and was drunken; and he was uncovered within his tent.
verses.bsb: But when he drank some of its wine, he became drunk and uncovered himself inside his tent.

verses.kjv: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father, and told his two brethren without.
verses.bsb: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw his father’s nakedness and told his two brothers outside.

verses.kjv: And Shem and Japheth took a garment, and laid it upon both their shoulders, and went backward, and covered the nakedness of their father; and their faces were backward, and they saw not their father’s nakedness.
verses.bsb: Then Shem and Japheth took a garment and placed it across their shoulders, and walking backward, they covered their father’s nakedness. Their faces were turned away so that they did not see their father’s nakedness.

verses.kjv: And Noah awoke from his wine, and knew what his younger son had done unto him.
verses.bsb: When Noah awoke from his drunkenness and learned what his youngest son had done to him,

verses.kjv: And he said, Cursed be Canaan; a servant of servants shall he be unto his brethren.
verses.bsb: he said,  “Cursed be Canaan! A servant of servants shall he be to his brothers.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Blessed be the Lord God of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.
verses.bsb: He also declared:  “Blessed be the LORD, the God of Shem! May Canaan be the servant of Shem.

verses.kjv: God shall enlarge Japheth, and he shall dwell in the tents of Shem; and Canaan shall be his servant.
verses.bsb: May God expand the territory of Japheth; may he dwell in the tents of Shem, and may Canaan be his servant.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Noah lived after the flood three hundred and fifty years.
verses.bsb: After the flood, Noah lived 350 years.

verses.kjv: And all the days of Noah were nine hundred and fifty years: and he died.
verses.bsb: So Noah lived a total of 950 years, and then he died.

verses.kjv: NOW these are the generations of the sons of Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth: and unto them were sons born after the flood.
verses.bsb: This is the account of Noah’s sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, who also had sons after the flood.

verses.kjv: The sons of Japheth; Gomer, and Magog, and Madai, and Javan, and Tubal, and Meshech, and Tiras.
verses.bsb: The sons of Japheth:  Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Gomer; Ashkenaz, and Riphath, and Togarmah.
verses.bsb: The sons of Gomer:  Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Javan; Elishah, and Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.
verses.bsb: And the sons of Javan:  Elishah, Tarshish, the Kittites, and the Rodanites.

verses.kjv: By these were the isles of the Gentiles divided in their lands; every one after his tongue, after their families, in their nations.
verses.bsb: From these, the maritime peoples separated into their territories, according to their languages, by clans within their nations.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the sons of Ham; Cush, and Mizraim, and Phut, and Canaan.
verses.bsb: The sons of Ham:  Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Cush; Seba, and Havilah, and Sabtah, and Raamah, and Sabtecha: and the sons of Raamah; Sheba, and Dedan.
verses.bsb: The sons of Cush:  Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca.  And the sons of Raamah:  Sheba and Dedan.

verses.kjv: And Cush begat Nimrod: he began to be a mighty one in the earth.
verses.bsb: Cush was the father of Nimrod, who began to be a mighty one on the earth.

verses.kjv: He was a mighty hunter before the Lord: wherefore it is said, Even as Nimrod the mighty hunter before the Lord.
verses.bsb: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD; so it is said, “Like Nimrod, a mighty hunter before the LORD.”

verses.kjv: And the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, and Erech, and Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
verses.bsb: His kingdom began in Babylon, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.

verses.kjv: Out of that land went forth Asshur, and builded Nineveh, and the city Rehoboth, and Calah,
verses.bsb: From that land he went forth into Assyria, where he built Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir, Calah,

verses.kjv: And Resen between Nineveh and Calah: the same is a great city.
verses.bsb: and Resen, which is between Nineveh and the great city of Calah.

verses.kjv: And Mizraim begat Ludim, and Anamim, and Lehabim, and Naphtuhim,
verses.bsb: Mizraim was the father of the Ludites, the Anamites, the Lehabites, the Naphtuhites,

verses.kjv: And Pathrusim, and Casluhim, (out of whom came Philistim,) and Caphtorim.
verses.bsb: the Pathrusites, the Casluhites (from whom the Philistines came), and the Caphtorites.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Canaan begat Sidon his firstborn, and Heth,
verses.bsb: And Canaan was the father of Sidon his firstborn, and of the Hittites,

verses.kjv: And the Jebusite, and the Amorite, and the Girgasite,
verses.bsb: the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,

verses.kjv: And the Hivite, and the Arkite, and the Sinite,
verses.bsb: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,

verses.kjv: And the Arvadite, and the Zemarite, and the Hamathite: and afterward were the families of the Canaanites spread abroad.
verses.bsb: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites.  Later the Canaanite clans were scattered,

verses.kjv: And the border of the Canaanites was from Sidon, as thou comest to Gerar, unto Gaza; as thou goest, unto Sodom, and Gomorrah, and Admah, and Zeboim, even unto Lasha.
verses.bsb: and the borders of Canaan extended from Sidon toward Gerar as far as Gaza, and then toward Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.

verses.kjv: These are the sons of Ham, after their families, after their tongues, in their countries, and in their nations.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Ham according to their clans, languages, lands, and nations.

verses.kjv: ¶ Unto Shem also, the father of all the children of Eber, the brother of Japheth the elder, even to him were children born.
verses.bsb: And sons were also born to Shem, the older brother of Japheth; Shem was the forefather of all the sons of Eber.

verses.kjv: The children of Shem; Elam, and Asshur, and Arphaxad, and Lud, and Aram.
verses.bsb: The sons of Shem:  Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram.

verses.kjv: And the children of Aram; Uz, and Hul, and Gether, and Mash.
verses.bsb: The sons of Aram:  Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.

verses.kjv: And Arphaxad begat Salah; and Salah begat Eber.
verses.bsb: Arphaxad was the father of Shelah, and Shelah was the father of Eber.

verses.kjv: And unto Eber were born two sons: the name of one was Peleg; for in his days was the earth divided; and his brother’s name was Joktan.
verses.bsb: Two sons were born to Eber: One was named Peleg, because in his days the earth was divided, and his brother was named Joktan.

verses.kjv: And Joktan begat Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah,
verses.bsb: And Joktan was the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,

verses.kjv: And Hadoram, and Uzal, and Diklah,
verses.bsb: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,

verses.kjv: And Obal, and Abimael, and Sheba,
verses.bsb: Obal, Abimael, Sheba,

verses.kjv: And Ophir, and Havilah, and Jobab: all these were the sons of Joktan.
verses.bsb: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were sons of Joktan.

verses.kjv: And their dwelling was from Mesha, as thou goest unto Sephar a mount of the east.
verses.bsb: Their territory extended from Mesha to Sephar, in the eastern hill country.

verses.kjv: These are the sons of Shem, after their families, after their tongues, in their lands, after their nations.
verses.bsb: These are the sons of Shem, according to their clans, languages, lands, and nations.

verses.kjv: These are the families of the sons of Noah, after their generations, in their nations: and by these were the nations divided in the earth after the flood.
verses.bsb: All these are the clans of Noah’s sons, according to their generations and nations. From these the nations of the earth spread out after the flood.

verses.kjv: AND the whole earth was of one language, and of one speech.
verses.bsb: Now the whole world had one language and a common form of speech.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as they journeyed from the east, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar; and they dwelt there.
verses.bsb: And as people journeyed eastward, they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.

verses.kjv: And they said one to another, Go to, let us make brick, and burn them throughly. And they had brick for stone, and slime had they for morter.
verses.bsb: And they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks and bake them thoroughly.” So they used brick instead of stone, and tar instead of mortar.

verses.kjv: And they said, Go to, let us build us a city and a tower, whose top may reach unto heaven; and let us make us a name, lest we be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.
verses.bsb: “Come,” they said, “let us build for ourselves a city with a tower that reaches to the heavens, that we may make a name for ourselves and not be scattered over the face of all the earth.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord came down to see the city and the tower, which the children of men builded.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD came down to see the city and the tower that the sons of men were building.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said, Behold, the people is one, and they have all one language; and this they begin to do: and now nothing will be restrained from them, which they have imagined to do.
verses.bsb: And the LORD said, “If they have begun to do this as one people speaking the same language, then nothing they devise will be beyond them.

verses.kjv: Go to, let us go down, and there confound their language, that they may not understand one another’s speech.
verses.bsb: Come, let Us go down and confuse their language, so that they will not understand one another’s speech.”

verses.kjv: So the Lord scattered them abroad from thence upon the face of all the earth: and they left off to build the city.
verses.bsb: So the LORD scattered them from there over the face of all the earth, and they stopped building the city.

verses.kjv: Therefore is the name of it called Babel; because the Lord did there confound the language of all the earth: and from thence did the Lord scatter them abroad upon the face of all the earth.
verses.bsb: That is why it is called Babel, for there the LORD confused the language of the whole world, and from that place the LORD scattered them over the face of all the earth.

verses.kjv: ¶ These are the generations of Shem: Shem was an hundred years old, and begat Arphaxad two years after the flood:
verses.bsb: This is the account of Shem. Two years after the flood, when Shem was 100 years old, he became the father of Arphaxad.

verses.kjv: And Shem lived after he begat Arphaxad five hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Arphaxad, Shem lived 500 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And Arphaxad lived five and thirty years, and begat Salah:
verses.bsb: When Arphaxad was 35 years old, he became the father of Shelah.

verses.kjv: And Arphaxad lived after he begat Salah four hundred and three years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Shelah, Arphaxad lived 403 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And Salah lived thirty years, and begat Eber:
verses.bsb: When Shelah was 30 years old, he became the father of Eber.

verses.kjv: And Salah lived after he begat Eber four hundred and three years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Eber, Shelah lived 403 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And Eber lived four and thirty years, and begat Peleg:
verses.bsb: When Eber was 34 years old, he became the father of Peleg.

verses.kjv: And Eber lived after he begat Peleg four hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Peleg, Eber lived 430 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And Peleg lived thirty years, and begat Reu:
verses.bsb: When Peleg was 30 years old, he became the father of Reu.

verses.kjv: And Peleg lived after he begat Reu two hundred and nine years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Reu, Peleg lived 209 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And Reu lived two and thirty years, and begat Serug:
verses.bsb: When Reu was 32 years old, he became the father of Serug.

verses.kjv: And Reu lived after he begat Serug two hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Serug, Reu lived 207 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And Serug lived thirty years, and begat Nahor:
verses.bsb: When Serug was 30 years old, he became the father of Nahor.

verses.kjv: And Serug lived after he begat Nahor two hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Nahor, Serug lived 200 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And Nahor lived nine and twenty years, and begat Terah:
verses.bsb: When Nahor was 29 years old, he became the father of Terah.

verses.kjv: And Nahor lived after he begat Terah an hundred and nineteen years, and begat sons and daughters.
verses.bsb: And after he had become the father of Terah, Nahor lived 119 years and had other sons and daughters.

verses.kjv: And Terah lived seventy years, and begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
verses.bsb: When Terah was 70 years old, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now these are the generations of Terah: Terah begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran begat Lot.
verses.bsb: This is the account of Terah. Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. And Haran became the father of Lot.

verses.kjv: And Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his nativity, in Ur of the Chaldees.
verses.bsb: During his father Terah’s lifetime, Haran died in his native land, in Ur of the Chaldeans.

verses.kjv: And Abram and Nahor took them wives: the name of Abram’s wife was Sarai; and the name of Nahor’s wife, Milcah, the daughter of Haran, the father of Milcah, and the father of Iscah.
verses.bsb: And Abram and Nahor took wives for themselves. Abram’s wife was named Sarai, and Nahor’s wife was named Milcah; she was the daughter of Haran, who was the father of both Milcah and Iscah.

verses.kjv: But Sarai was barren; she had no child.
verses.bsb: But Sarai was barren; she had no children.

verses.kjv: And Terah took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Haran his son’s son, and Sarai his daughter in law, his son Abram’s wife; and they went forth with them from Ur of the Chaldees, to go into the land of Canaan; and they came unto Haran, and dwelt there.
verses.bsb: And Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai the wife of Abram, and they set out from Ur of the Chaldeans for the land of Canaan. But when they arrived in Haran, they settled there.

verses.kjv: And the days of Terah were two hundred and five years: and Terah died in Haran.
verses.bsb: Terah lived 205 years, and he died in Haran.

verses.kjv: NOW the Lord had said unto Abram, Get thee out of thy country, and from thy kindred, and from thy father’s house, unto a land that I will shew thee:
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Abram, “Leave your country, your kindred, and your father’s household, and go to the land I will show you.

verses.kjv: And I will make of thee a great nation, and I will bless thee, and make thy name great; and thou shalt be a blessing:
verses.bsb: I will make you into a great nation, and I will bless you; I will make your name great, so that you will be a blessing.

verses.kjv: And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse him that curseth thee: and in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed.
verses.bsb: I will bless those who bless you and curse those who curse you; and all the families of the earth will be blessed through you.”

verses.kjv: So Abram departed, as the Lord had spoken unto him; and Lot went with him: and Abram was seventy and five years old when he departed out of Haran.
verses.bsb: So Abram departed, as the LORD had directed him, and Lot went with him. Abram was seventy-five years old when he left Haran.

verses.kjv: And Abram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother’s son, and all their substance that they had gathered, and the souls that they had gotten in Haran; and they went forth to go into the land of Canaan; and into the land of Canaan they came.
verses.bsb: And Abram took his wife Sarai, his nephew Lot, and all the possessions and people they had acquired in Haran, and set out for the land of Canaan.  When they came to the land of Canaan,

verses.kjv: ¶ And Abram passed through the land unto the place of Sichem, unto the plain of Moreh. And the Canaanite was then in the land.
verses.bsb: Abram traveled through the land as far as the site of the Oak of Moreh at Shechem. And at that time the Canaanites were in the land.

verses.kjv: And the Lord appeared unto Abram, and said, Unto thy seed will I give this land: and there builded he an altar unto the Lord, who appeared unto him.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD appeared to Abram and said, “I will give this land to your offspring.” So Abram built an altar there to the LORD, who had appeared to him.

verses.kjv: And he removed from thence unto a mountain on the east of Beth-el, and pitched his tent, having Beth-el on the west, and Hai on the east: and there he builded an altar unto the Lord, and called upon the name of the Lord.
verses.bsb: From there Abram moved on to the hill country east of Bethel and pitched his tent, with Bethel to the west and Ai to the east. There he built an altar to the LORD, and he called on the name of the LORD.

verses.kjv: And Abram journeyed, going on still toward the south.
verses.bsb: And Abram journeyed on toward the Negev.

verses.kjv: ¶ And there was a famine in the land: and Abram went down into Egypt to sojourn there; for the famine was grievous in the land.
verses.bsb: Now there was a famine in the land. So Abram went down to Egypt to live there for a while because the famine was severe.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when he was come near to enter into Egypt, that he said unto Sarai his wife, Behold now, I know that thou art a fair woman to look upon:
verses.bsb: As he was about to enter Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai, “Look, I know that you are a beautiful woman,

verses.kjv: Therefore it shall come to pass, when the Egyptians shall see thee, that they shall say, This is his wife: and they will kill me, but they will save thee alive.
verses.bsb: and when the Egyptians see you, they will say, ‘This is his wife.’ Then they will kill me but will let you live.

verses.kjv: Say, I pray thee, thou art my sister: that it may be well with me for thy sake; and my soul shall live because of thee.
verses.bsb: Please say you are my sister, so that I will be treated well for your sake, and on account of you my life will be spared.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And it came to pass, that, when Abram was come into Egypt, the Egyptians beheld the woman that she was very fair.
verses.bsb: So when Abram entered Egypt, the Egyptians saw that the woman was very beautiful.

verses.kjv: The princes also of Pharaoh saw her, and commended her before Pharaoh: and the woman was taken into Pharaoh’s house.
verses.bsb: When Pharaoh’s officials saw Sarai, they commended her to him, and she was taken into the palace of Pharaoh.

verses.kjv: And he entreated Abram well for her sake: and he had sheep, and oxen, and he asses, and menservants, and maidservants, and she asses, and camels.
verses.bsb: He treated Abram well on her account, and Abram acquired sheep and cattle, male and female donkeys, menservants and maidservants, and camels.

verses.kjv: And the Lord plagued Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai Abram’s wife.
